assessment_items.question_content:
**This portrait shows the artist fully frontal, which was a pose that was generally reserved for [[☃ dropdown 1]].**



![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/eabfebf7b3fcf9d234b01f56ea41e3c6.png)
hints: **This portrait shows the artist fully frontal, in the same pose that is reserved for Christ.**

assessment_items.question_content:
**The frontality of the portrait shows humility and piety.**



![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/eabfebf7b3fcf9d234b01f56ea41e3c6.png)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: **The frontality of the portrait is not the most flattering, but it is direct and powerful (not humble and pious).**

assessment_items.question_content:
**Dürer embraced medieval ideas and was interested in the way that faith and fear worked together.**



![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/eabfebf7b3fcf9d234b01f56ea41e3c6.png)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: **Dürer was a Humanist and was interested in the way that science and art worked together.**

assessment_items.question_content:
**Dürer studied art in [[☃ dropdown 1]] and brought his knowledge back to his home country of [[☃ dropdown 2]].**



![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/eabfebf7b3fcf9d234b01f56ea41e3c6.png)
hints: **Dürer studied art in Italy and brought his knowledge of Italian art back to Germany.**
